Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Brian McMahon
Message:
fix: mock S3/boto3 calls in tests for CI (no AWS credentials)

- veto_analysis: change remaining _load_predictions_for_dates mocks to
  _load_all_predictions (code calls the latter which hits S3 first)
- regression_monitor: mock rollback_all in tests that detect regression
  (regression triggers auto-rollback which calls S3)
- backtest: mock signal_loader.load in test_no_stale_flag_proceeds
  (simulation loop loads signals from S3 when signals_by_date is None)
- flow_doctor_smoke: use dict key pattern (api_password) instead of
  AWS key pattern to test scrubbing without triggering secret scanner

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Brian McMahon
Message:
feat: re-enable flow-doctor via bundled source install (Phase 1)

Remove PyPI dependency (was breaking EC2 pip install for 4+ days). Install
flow-doctor from cloned source in spot_backtest.sh. Add fd.report() to 7
additional failure paths (simulation, executor optimizer, veto analysis,
research optimizer, predictor backtest, sizing A/B). Add 9 integration smoke
tests for capture, dedup, scrubbing, and multi-site error reporting. Fix 4
pre-existing test failures (price_loader ffill, veto_analysis mock target).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
